query: Meghalaya Rajya Sabha election 2026 NPP nominee announcement
answer:
## Meghalaya Rajya Sabha Election 2026 – NPP Nominee Announcement: Research Report

### Election Overview

The **Election Commission of India (ECI)** announced on **May 22, 2026** that biennial elections to Rajya Sabha seats across multiple states — including **one seat from Meghalaya** — will be held on **June 18, 2026**. The seat is being vacated due to the expiry of the term of **Dr. W.R. Kharlukhi (NPP)**, who was elected in June 2020.

---

### NPP / MDA-II Nominee Announcement

On **June 3, 2026**, the ruling **MDA-II (Meghalaya Democratic Alliance - II)** coalition officially announced **James PK Sangma** as their candidate for the Rajya Sabha seat. The decision was made at an MDA-II meeting chaired by **Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma**.

**James PK Sangma's profile:**
- **Brother of CM Conrad Sangma** and **son of the late P.A. Sangma** (former Lok Sabha Speaker and founder of NPP)
- Currently serves as **National Working President of NPP** (since March 2025)
- **Former Rajya Sabha MP** (2008–2014)
- **Former Meghalaya Cabinet Minister** (2018–2021)
- Lost his assembly seat in the 2023 state elections
- Expected to file nomination papers on **June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM**

---

### Election Schedule

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| May 22, 2026 | ECI announces election date |
| June 3, 2026 | MDA-II announces James PK Sangma as candidate |
| June 5, 2026 | Expected nomination filing (2:30 PM) |
| June 18, 2026 | Polling day (9 AM–4 PM); counting at 5 PM |
| June 20, 2026 | Process completion deadline |

---

### Retiring Member

**Dr. Wanweiroy Kharlukhi (WR Kharlukhi)** of NPP:
- Elected to Rajya Sabha on June 22, 2020
- Won the 2020 election defeating Congress candidate Kennedy Khyriem by 20 votes (39 vs. 19 out of 59 votes polled)
- His 6-year term expires in 2026

---

### Assembly Composition (The Electorate)

Rajya Sabha members are elected by state MLAs. The 60-member Meghalaya Legislative Assembly currently breaks down as:

**MDA-II (Ruling Coalition) — 51 seats:**

| Party | Seats |
|-------|-------|
| NPP (National People's Party) | 33 |
| UDP (United Democratic Party) | 12 |
| BJP (Bharatiya Janata Party) | 2 |
| HSPDP (Hill State People's Democratic Party) | 2 |
| Independents (supporting govt) | 2 |
| **Total** | **51** |

**Opposition — 9 seats:**

| Party | Seats |
|-------|-------|
| AITC (TMC) | 5 |
| VPP (Voice of the People Party) | 4 |
| **Total** | **9** |

With 51 of 60 seats, the MDA-II alliance has an overwhelming majority (only 31 votes needed to win). The outcome is virtually certain in favor of James PK Sangma.

---

### Opposition Status

As of June 5, 2026, **no opposition candidate has been publicly announced**. Given the ruling alliance's commanding 85% of assembly seats, any opposition candidacy would be largely symbolic.

---

### Political Context

- The NPP has grown from 26 seats (2023 election) to 33 currently, likely through defections and by-elections
- The Sangma family's political dominance continues — CM Conrad Sangma and brother James PK Sangma
- The Indian National Congress, which fielded a candidate in 2020, appears absent from contention in 2026
- The 2020 RS election pattern (NPP-led coalition comfortably securing the seat) is expected to repeat

key_facts: The ECI announced on May 22, 2026 that the Meghalaya Rajya Sabha election will be held on June 18, 2026, for one seat.
On June 3, 2026, the ruling MDA-II coalition announced James PK Sangma as their candidate for the Rajya Sabha seat.
James PK Sangma is the brother of CM Conrad K. Sangma, son of late Lok Sabha Speaker P.A. Sangma, and NPP National Working President since March 2025.
James Sangma is a former Rajya Sabha MP (2008-2014) and former Meghalaya Cabinet Minister (2018-2021) who lost his assembly seat in the 2023 state elections.
The retiring member is Dr. W.R. Kharlukhi (NPP), elected in June 2020, whose 6-year term expires in 2026.
The MDA-II coalition holds 51 of 60 assembly seats (85%), making the outcome virtually certain — only 31 votes are needed to win.
NPP holds 33 seats, UDP 12, BJP 2, HSPDP 2, and 2 supporting Independents in the ruling coalition.
The opposition (AITC: 5 seats, VPP: 4 seats) has not announced any candidate as of June 5, 2026.
James PK Sangma's nomination filing was expected on June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM.
